Title:
Title-Source: body_internal|generated
No-Joke-Found: false

In ancient times, in the land of Goria, there lived a peaceful tribe of
people whose exalted leader carried the title of "The Path to Wisdom and
Enlightenment" but all of those who knew him called him simply The Path. 

The Path had three wives and it was his duty to provide an heir to his
position by siring a son (this was before political, racial, and gender
correctness) to pass his wisdom and leadership skills to.  However, years
went by and there was no heir apparent and apparently, no heir in sight. In
desperation, The Path decided that a change of venue might help in his
heir-raising endeavor so he built three new tepees for his lovely wives.
One he made out of buffalo skin, one was fashioned from elk skin, but the
third was made from imported hippopotamus skin. For whatever reason, all
three wives became pregnant almost immediately and at the same time.

Nine months later, the wife living in the buffalo skin tepee gave birth to a
beautiful baby girl. The next day, the wife in the elk skin tepee delivered
a baby girl, as well. On the third day, the wife in the hippo skin tepee
gave birth to twin baby boys. The Path, being the intellectual leader of his
people was deeply interested in the phenomenon of the different dwellings
and the affect it had on child birth. He quickly developed a theory that has
stood the test of time. The Path, the Gorian theory states that the squaw of
the hippopotamus is equal to the sum of the squaws of the other two hides.
